column1: TROPICS | NHC continues to update the area they expect development with this tropical wave as the models shift west. After highlighting areas east of Florida yesterday, the latest model shift west has the NHC now looking into the eastern Gulf. Any development likely not occurring until the weekend at the earliest. (Note: I mentioned a comparison to Elena in 1985 on DPL last night.  Too early to know for sure, but whatever forms could easily stall and possibly even loop in the Northern Gulf. We could be dealing with a messy rainmaker for quite awhile next week)

For some of us, even those who do not live in flood zones, our homes, streets, etc. do flood. I wish that wasn't the case but we are not lucky enuf to live in hurricane/stormproof homes. In Sarasota, during the last rain event that happened about a month ago, we had huge flooding issues in a new house we moved to even tho it's way out of the flood zone. There's nowhere for that much water to go. So, unfortunately some of us do panic and feel anxious during heavy rainstorms like this one coming our way.

This is a larger issue, if normal summer storm impact you, then that is a city planning issue, allowing buildings and not accounting for storm drainage. P.S. I am sorry that is happening at your new house.
The rain event in June wasn’t a normal summer storm. It was a 1000yr storm in Sarasota. Design standards are typically for a 100yr storm.
